changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for specificity
review_notes: The functions generally map well to Kubernetes security controls relevant to the control objective of identifying and assessing risks. However, the audit logging function name was too generic and has been updated for clarity and specificity.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names specify the resource they pertain to, such as 'apiserver' for audit logging.
title: CC3.1 COSO Principle 6: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives
step1_reasoning: The control focuses on specifying objectives with clarity to identify and assess risks. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that access controls are enforced with least privilege (RBAC), audit logging is enabled for tracking and assessing actions, network policies are in place to restrict ingress traffic by default, and secrets are encrypted at rest to mitigate risks related to sensitive data exposure.
